title: Perspective mysticism or advanced science
wikitext:
[]: Perspective-mysticism or advanced science             Quote Post by n2eva » Fri Aug 12, 2011 11:01 pm I had come across this great site in the past and again recently. It seems the right place to hear about other fans' views on something. I remember reading in the past and here again about the First Ancestral Race. This made wonder about what it is that humanity is mapping as far as the prophecies of the Dead Sea Scrolls. Is it humans using religion and mysticism to understand something truly mystic of alien origin or using using religion and mysticism to understand a super advanced science(that appears mystic) of alien origin? Personally I think it's the former. I should mention that I have no bias as far as one over the other in storytelling. Both are cool but in Evangelion I think it's humanity understanding something truly mystic in nature. Just curious as to what others' opinions/points might be. Even the concept of supremely advanced science/mysticism being "one and the same" may apply. Is there a clarity on this or is it left to the viewer in what is one of the things that makes anime great-they don't tell you everything? My bad if my question on the concept isn't clear. Thanks

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 12, 2011 11:26 pm Clarke's Third Law is in full effect here. The First Ancestral Race basically used super-science, but it's basically close enough to magic from our perspective.

[]:             Quote Post by n2eva » Fri Aug 12, 2011 11:34 pm Good point. I'd actually heard of that and considered it applicable in this case.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenshin Sephiroth » Sat Aug 13, 2011 5:14 am When interviewed by Ben Stein on Intelligent Design, Richard Dawkins hypothesized that perhaps "at some earlier time, somewhere in the universe, a civilization evolved, probably by some kind of Darwinian means, probably to a very high level of technology, and designed a form of life that they seeded onto perhaps this planet." ( Source ) Notice that he stresses the non-mystical nature of these proposed designers. I've always thought of the FAR in the same vein: not gods, but an insanely advanced life-form beyond human comprehension charged with the spurring of organic evolution throughout galaxies (think the alien designer civilization from Clarke's 2001 ). The Dead Sea Scrolls is merely a "secular" manual, not a mystic text, that is nevertheless interpreted religiously by SEELE, evidently due to them originally being a religious cult in the Dark Ages, according to the Classified Information.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Aug 13, 2011 5:30 am Kenshin Sephiroth wrote: When interviewed by Ben Stein on Intelligent Design, Richard Dawkins hypothesized that perhaps "at some earlier time, somewhere in the universe, a civilization evolved, probably by some kind of Darwinian means, probably to a very high level of technology, and designed a form of life that they seeded onto perhaps this planet." ( Source ) Notice that he stresses the non-mystical nature of these proposed designers. I've always thought of the FAR in the same vein: not gods, but an insanely advanced life form beyond human comprehension charged with the spurring of organic evolution throughout galaxies (think the alien designer civilization in Clarke's 2001 ). The Dead Sea Scrolls is merely a "secular" manual, not a mystic text, that is nevertheless interpreted religiously by SEELE, evidently due to them originally being a religious cult in the Dark Ages, according to the Classified Information.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenshin Sephiroth » Sat Aug 13, 2011 6:07 am CJD wrote: I wonder whether Seele truly interprets them as religious. They apply christian names to them, but that could just be in there roots as, as you say, a religious cult. Surely, a body this intelligent, with the information they have (Dead Sea Scrolls), is aware that it's science and not religious. (Not critiquing religion, don't get the wrong idea please). Very likely, upon discovery and soon after, they thought they were mystical, but as they further translated or interpreted them I'd wager they realized their true origins.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Aug 13, 2011 7:07 am I'm still curious, though, whether that's implying religion or just zealotry. When I read that the first time I took it to mean their "Faith in the ability to achieve divinity" was restored. I think it comes down to how much about the FAR was in the DSS. I dunno, honestly I'm not sure one way or the other. It's a moot point, though, because, to take what Aura said a step further, I think zealotry to an idea is indistinguishable from zealotry to religion.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Aug 13, 2011 2:09 pm At the soft ends of the scale, the difference between sci/fi and fantasy (ie. science and teh magic), is how they try to explain it. Magic and or religion would be stuff that "just is that way" and "you just have to trust/believe in it", science-y stuff comes with a "logical explanation". It can be ludicrous technobabble that wouldn't work in real life, but the in-universe explanation requires no supernatural elements. EVA was propbably deliberately placed in the "grey zone" ("sufficiently advanced technology", technology/biological things named/labeled after mysthical things, and visce-versa), probably to make it all more interesting/Epic In the end, most is ultimately explained though biology/technology (The FAR are treated as "aliens", the story isn't told in a creation-myth style that uses formulation likes "The great old ones" or stuff, many of the things that have mythology-ish names are just technology/biology stuff named that way.) Even the prophecies might as well be interpreted to be of the self-fulfilling sort. Clear, undesputable supernatural elements without explanation are the soul (which is treated as very real thing that you can do stuff with, e.g. fill it into an EVA.), and the AT-Field, tough this is, once again blurring the lines, always "sufficiently analized magic" in the sense that they measure that stuff, they integrate it into/work on it with technology, and generally apply the scientific method to things of a magic-y origin like we do it with our "real" natural laws. One could almost say that they simply live in an universe where souls simply are tangible, analyzable stuff and can pe proven to exist just like, i dunno, atoms can be proven in our world. Their universe clearly does not work like the real life one, but the "thinking methods" are mostly science-y: Stuff is analizes, probed, built, created, and that yields results, the human mind is a realistically limited, but useful tool here. (as oposed to the romanticism/magic spectrum which puts more emphasis on emotion/believing - Emotions in the EVa-verse have no more effect that "regular" emotions and are themselves treated as "analyzable" (psychology motifs))

[]:             Quote Post by n2eva » Sat Aug 13, 2011 11:09 pm With regards to Kenshin Sepiroth and CJD's discussion-interesting views on Seele's interpretations of the FAR's science. Maybe Seele does realize these things are of an advanced science, but in relation to the religions in this vein of the masses they of course differ. An angel in the classic sense developed through the ages as opposed to Seele's true core definition of an "angel", in the alien technological sense. A scientifically engineered super entity with its unique psychology(Kaworu). Classic divinity(celestial hierarchy) or the true divinity(Instrumentality/final evolution)-Seele's knowledge with respects towards the true definitions of these thing as opposed to the rest of the world's. I'm only speaking within the context of this story and mean no offense to religion in general. Kendrix makes an interesting point as well for actual mysticism as opposed to science. Maybe the FAR's science allowed them to access a mystic realm-or humans using science to coax things that are mystical. I may be stretching it with these ideas but I am starting to lean towards super science as the basis instead of mysticism as posted originally. Everyone's input is much appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Aug 14, 2011 8:02 am Kendrix wrote: One could almost say that they simply live in an universe where souls simply are tangible, analyzable stuff and can pe proven to exist just like, i dunno, atoms can be proven in our world.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 14, 2011 4:11 pm While souls aren't touchable, they are proven to exist in Eva, and we see that proof throughout the series and EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Aug 14, 2011 5:04 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Er...they are very much touchable. The universe of Eva has an entire branch of science based around messing with the soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenshin Sephiroth » Mon Aug 15, 2011 11:13 am Perhaps the soul in NGE isn't something tangible, but rather something intangible connected to something tangible, similar to how a soul is connected to an Eva. The philospher of mind René Descartes was convinced that the soul of each individual resides within the pineal gland ; an organic, physical place of incubation functioning as the "principal seat of the soul"; this conviction being very reminiscent to how Rei describes the entry plug in each Eva as a "throne of the soul" in her poem during Ep 14. Maybe these bodily containers carrying the soul is what allows Nerv to extract them from humans and implant them in the Evas?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Aug 15, 2011 12:15 pm Kenshin Sephiroth wrote: Perhaps the soul in NGE isn't something tangible, but rather something intangible connected to something tangible, similar to how a soul is connected to an Eva. The philospher of mind René Descartes was convinced that the soul of each individual resides within the pineal gland ; an organic, physical place of incubation functioning as the "principal seat of the soul"; this conviction being very reminiscent to how Rei describes the entry plug in each Eva as a "throne of the soul" in her poem during Ep 14. Maybe these bodily containers carrying the soul is what allows Nerv to extract them from humans and implant them in the Evas?

[]:             Quote Post by n2eva » Mon Aug 15, 2011 7:37 pm Yeah, I see what everyone's saying and I agree now that the soul too is scientific and not mystical-used by both the FAR and humans. Not a situation of science coaxing something mystical but highly advanced technology through and through. Seele knows that all these things are alien science in essence but still regard them with weighted holy reverence because they are amazing and/or of how they relate to humanity. I should explain that finding out about the FAR didn't change my view on the tone of this show but did slighty put a different spin on things. I do now think it's super science not mysticism.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:05 pm Re: SEELE's intention/goals - This seems to sum it up rather well (politics not intended):

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Thu Aug 18, 2011 4:21 pm Depending on exactly how old the FAR were, and how long ago they sent out the Seeds, a pseudo-mystic understanding of their technology might be preferable to treating it as science -- imagine if the FAR had evolved in the immediate aftermath of the Big Bang, and all their science and evolution relied upon a universe that was smaller and hotter than our present, after 14/24+ billion years of the universe expanding at super-luminal speed. Adam and Lilith could be picked apart for centuries by human scientists, extrapolating the Seed biology back to the circumstance in which they were created. Gendo and SEELE ain't looking to verify the state of the universe +150,000,000 years after the Big Bang. They're trying to use something they don't have time to fully understand. Treating the origin of Evangelion technology as an unknown, possibly irrelevant super-science would probably drive everyone involved crazy.
